description: Consolidated monitor DDC/CI VCP-command database. Per-monitor mappings for brightness (VCP 0x10) and power-off (VCP 0xD6 DPMS or 0xE1 Power) plus quirks. Lookup key is the 7-character EDID identifier: 3-letter PnP manufacturer ID concatenated with the 4-hex-digit product code (e.g. 'DEL4187' for Dell P2719HC).

sources.name: rockowitz/ddcutil
sources.contribution: VCP feature codes reference + per-model quirks

sources.name: Informatic/python-ddcci
sources.contribution: Raw I2C wire format / 60ms throttle reference

designNotes: Brightness: VCP 0x10 (Luminance) is universal across all 309 ddccontrol-db monitors and all surveyed implementations. No monitor in the corpus uses a different brightness code. VCP 0x13 (Backlight) is deprecated by VESA per MCCS 2.2 and is not used by any of the implementations surveyed.

Power-off: There are TWO competing MCCS conventions:
  1. VCP 0xD6 (DPMS / Power Mode) with values {1=On, 2=Standby, 3=Suspend, 4=Soft-Off, 5=Hard-Off}.
     0x05 is the universal write-only 'hard off' equivalent to the front-panel button.
  2. VCP 0xE1 (vendor 'Power') with values {1=On, 0=Off} per VESA default - but Dell P-series and U-series monitors INVERT this to {0=On, 1=Off}.
Strategy: prefer 0xD6=5 because it is the most universally-supported. Fall back to 0xE1 if the monitor's caps string omits 0xD6. Always honor the per-monitor 'isInverted' flag.

Identification: lookup key is EDID PnP manufacturer ID (3 letters, encoded in EDID bytes 8-9) concatenated with the 16-bit product code (EDID bytes 10-11), formatted as 7 uppercase chars (e.g. 'DEL4187'). This matches the ddccontrol-db filename convention.

Inheritance: monitors in ddccontrol-db can inherit controls via <include file="X"/>. We resolved the include chain at extraction time: each monitor's powerOff array contains ALL effective power commands (inline OR inherited from VESA / family templates).

vcpReference.name: Degauss
vcpReference.desc: Causes a CRT to perform a degauss cycle. CRT-only.

vcpReference.name: New control value
vcpReference.desc: Indicates that a display user control (other than power) has been used to change and save (or autosave) a new value.

vcpReference.name: Soft controls
vcpReference.desc: Allows display controls to be used as soft keys.

vcpReference.name: Restore factory defaults
vcpReference.desc: Restore all factory presets including brightness/contrast, geometry, color, and TV defaults.

vcpReference.name: Restore factory brightness/contrast defaults
vcpReference.desc: Restore factory defaults for brightness and contrast.

vcpReference.name: Restore factory geometry defaults
vcpReference.desc: Restore factory defaults for geometry adjustments.

vcpReference.name: Restore color defaults
vcpReference.desc: Restore factory defaults for color settings.

vcpReference.name: Restore factory TV defaults
vcpReference.desc: Restore factory defaults for TV functions.

vcpReference.name: Color temperature increment
vcpReference.desc: Color temperature increment used by feature 0Ch Color Temperature Request.

vcpReference.name: Color temperature request
vcpReference.desc: Specifies a color temperature (degrees Kelvin).

vcpReference.name: Clock
vcpReference.desc: Increase/decrease the sampling clock frequency.

vcpReference.name: Brightness
vcpReference.desc: Increase/decrease the brightness of the image. Renamed 'Luminosity' in MCCS 3.0.
vcpReference.notes: Primary brightness code. Most commonly used VCP for brightness control.

vcpReference.name: Flesh tone enhancement
vcpReference.desc: Select contrast enhancement algorithm respecting flesh tone region. Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Contrast
vcpReference.desc: Increase/decrease the contrast of the image.

vcpReference.name: Backlight control
vcpReference.desc: Increase/decrease the specified backlight control value. Deprecated in MCCS 2.2 but still implemented on some V2.2 monitors.
vcpReference.notes: Sometimes used as alternative to 0x10 for true backlight control independent of pixel-domain brightness.

vcpReference.name: Select color preset
vcpReference.desc: Select a specified color temperature. Interpretation varies by VCP version (SIMPLE_NC v2.0; COMPLEX_NC v3.0/2.2).

vcpReference.name: Video gain: Red
vcpReference.desc: Increase/decrease the luminescence of red pixels.

vcpReference.name: User color vision compensation
vcpReference.desc: Increase/decrease degree of color vision compensation. Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Video gain: Green
vcpReference.desc: Increase/decrease the luminescence of green pixels.

vcpReference.name: Video gain: Blue
vcpReference.desc: Increase/decrease the luminescence of blue pixels.

vcpReference.name: Focus
vcpReference.desc: Increase/decrease the focus of the image.

vcpReference.name: Auto setup
vcpReference.desc: Perform autosetup function (H/V position, clock, clock phase, A/D converter, etc.).

vcpReference.name: Auto color setup
vcpReference.desc: Perform color autosetup function (R/G/B gain and offset, A/D setup, etc.).

vcpReference.name: Horizontal Convergence M/G
vcpReference.desc: Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Gray scale expansion
vcpReference.desc: Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Vertical Convergence M/G
vcpReference.desc: Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Horizontal Parallelogram
vcpReference.desc: Was 'Key Balance' in MCCS 2.0.

vcpReference.name: Vertical Parallelogram
vcpReference.desc: Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Horizontal Keystone
vcpReference.desc: Was 'Horizontal Trapezoid' in MCCS 2.0.

vcpReference.name: Vertical Keystone
vcpReference.desc: Was 'Vertical Trapezoid' in MCCS 2.0.

vcpReference.name: Rotation
vcpReference.desc: Was 'Tilt (rotation)' in MCCS 2.0.

vcpReference.name: Active control
vcpReference.desc: Read id of one feature that has changed; 0x00 indicates no more.

vcpReference.name: Performance Preservation
vcpReference.desc: Controls features aimed at preserving display performance. Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: 6 axis saturation: Red
vcpReference.notes: Spec defines as 0x00..0xff with 0x7F nominal; Dell U3011 returns nominal=50 max=100. ddcutil treats 0x59..0x5E as standard continuous because of this.

vcpReference.name: Input Source
vcpReference.desc: Selects active video source. Standard values: 01=VGA-1, 02=VGA-2, 03=DVI-1, 04=DVI-2, 05=Composite-1, 06=Composite-2, 07=S-Video-1, 08=S-Video-2, 09..0B=Tuners, 0C..0E=Component video. v2.2 adds 0F=DP-1, 10=DP-2, 11=HDMI-1, 12=HDMI-2.
vcpReference.notes: Many monitors use vendor-specific values outside this range. Capability strings often expose the vendor's specific input mappings.

vcpReference.name: Audio speaker volume
vcpReference.desc: Adjusts speaker volume. In v3.0+, 0x00 and 0xFF reserved; 0x01..0xFE is continuous range.

vcpReference.name: Speaker Select
vcpReference.desc: Selects a group of speakers.

vcpReference.name: Ambient light sensor
vcpReference.notes: Defined in MCCS 2.1, but observed on NEC PA241W which reports VCP version as 2.0.

vcpReference.name: Backlight Level: White
vcpReference.desc: First defined in MCCS 2.2.

vcpReference.name: Backlight Level: Red
vcpReference.desc: First defined in MCCS 2.2.

vcpReference.name: Backlight Level: Green
vcpReference.desc: First defined in MCCS 2.2.

vcpReference.name: Backlight Level: Blue
vcpReference.desc: First defined in MCCS 2.2.

vcpReference.name: Gamma
vcpReference.desc: Select relative or absolute gamma. Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Display Identification Operation
vcpReference.desc: Was 'EDID operation'. Causes a selected 128 byte block of EDID to be read.

vcpReference.name: Adjust Focal Plane
vcpReference.desc: Deprecated in MCCS 3.0/2.2.

vcpReference.name: Trapezoid
vcpReference.desc: Deprecated in MCCS 3.0/2.2.

vcpReference.name: Keystone
vcpReference.desc: Deprecated as of MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Color Saturation
vcpReference.desc: Was 'TV Color Saturation' in v2.0.

vcpReference.name: Audio mute/Screen blank
vcpReference.desc: Mute/unmute audio, and (v2.2) screen blank.

vcpReference.name: Audio Processor Mode
vcpReference.desc: Was 'Audio Stereo Mode' in v2.0.

vcpReference.name: Window control on/off
vcpReference.desc: Deprecated in v3.0/2.2.

vcpReference.name: 6 axis hue control: Red
vcpReference.notes: Per MCCS spec values are 0..255 with 127 nominal; Dell U3011 returns nominal=50 max=100. ddcutil treats 0x9B..0xA0 as standard continuous.

vcpReference.name: Window mask control
vcpReference.desc: Turn selected window operation on/off, window mask.

vcpReference.name: Horizontal frequency
vcpReference.desc: Horizontal sync signal frequency as determined by the display.

vcpReference.name: Vertical frequency
vcpReference.desc: Vertical sync signal frequency.

vcpReference.name: Settings
vcpReference.desc: Store/restore the user saved values for the current mode.

vcpReference.name: Source Timing Mode
vcpReference.desc: Indicates timing mode being sent by host.

vcpReference.name: Monitor status
vcpReference.desc: Video mode and status of a DPVL capable monitor.

vcpReference.name: Display usage time
vcpReference.desc: Active power on time in hours.

vcpReference.name: Application enable key
vcpReference.desc: 2-byte value used to allow an application to only operate with known products.

vcpReference.name: Display controller type
vcpReference.desc: Mfg id of controller and 2 byte mfg-specific controller type. Includes table of known controllers (Conexant, Genesis, Macronix, IDT, Mstar, Myson, Phillips, PixelWorks, RealTek, Sage, Silicon Image, SmartASIC, STMicro, Topro, Trumpion, Welltrend, Samsung, Novatek, STK, Silicon Optics, TI, Analogix, Quantum Data, NXP, Chrontel, Parade, THine, Trident, Micros).

vcpReference.name: Display firmware level
vcpReference.desc: 2 byte firmware level.

vcpReference.name: OSD/Button Control
vcpReference.desc: Sets and indicates the current operational state of OSD (and buttons in v2.2).

vcpReference.name: OSD Language
vcpReference.desc: On Screen Display language.

vcpReference.name: Status Indicators
vcpReference.desc: Control up to 16 LED indicators. Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Asset Tag
vcpReference.desc: Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Power mode
vcpReference.desc: DPM and DPMS status. Standard values: 0x01=DPM On / DPMS Off (normal on); 0x02=DPM Off / DPMS Standby; 0x03=DPM Off / DPMS Suspend; 0x04=DPM Off / DPMS Off (true power off); 0x05=Write-only value to turn off display.
vcpReference.notes: Primary code for software display power-off. Not all monitors implement 0x04/0x05; some treat 0x04 as soft-off and require 0x05 for hard-off.

vcpReference.name: Auxiliary power output
vcpReference.desc: Controls aux power from display to host device.

vcpReference.name: Scan mode
vcpReference.desc: Was 'Scan format' in v2.0. Values: 00=Normal, 01=Underscan, 02=Overscan, 03=Widescreen (v2.0 only).

vcpReference.name: Image Mode
vcpReference.desc: Added in MCCS 2.1.

vcpReference.name: Display Application
vcpReference.desc: Was 'Display Mode'. Values: 00=Standard, 01=Productivity, 02=Mixed, 03=Movie, 04=User defined, 05=Games, 06=Sports, 07=Professional (signal processing disabled), 08=Standard intermediate power, 09=Standard low power, 0A=Demonstration, F0=Dynamic contrast.

vcpReference.name: Scratch Pad
vcpReference.desc: Was 'Operation Mode' in v2.0.

vcpReference.name: VCP Version
vcpReference.desc: MCCS version (high byte=major, low byte=minor).

quirks: Power command 0xE1 uses INVERTED semantics (off=1, on=0). Sending the VESA-default 0xE1=0 to this monitor would turn it ON, not off - use 0xD6=5 (DPMS hard off) or 0xE1=1 instead.
Power command 0xE1 uses INVERTED semantics (off=1, on=0). Sending the VESA-default 0xE1=0 to this monitor would turn it ON, not off - use 0xD6=5 (DPMS hard off) or 0xE1=1 instead.
Power command 0xE1 uses INVERTED semantics (off=1, on=0). Sending the VESA-default 0xE1=0 to this monitor would turn it ON, not off - use 0xD6=5 (DPMS hard off) or 0xE1=1 instead.
Power command 0xE1 uses INVERTED semantics (off=1, on=0). Sending the VESA-default 0xE1=0 to this monitor would turn it ON, not off - use 0xD6=5 (DPMS hard off) or 0xE1=1 instead.

quirks: [ddcutil] Returns 'nominal' value 50 / max value 100 for 6-axis saturation (0x59-0x5E) and 6-axis hue (0x9B-0xA0). Spec defines nominal=127 / max=255. - Treat 6-axis saturation/hue as standard continuous (not 'value < 127 decreases / > 127 increases'). format_feature_detail_6_axis_hue() exists but is not used.
[ddcutil] All-zero response when reading unsupported table feature instead of error code. - DDCRC_READ_ALL_ZERO is treated as indication of unsupported feature, not retried.

[ddcutil] Feature 0x52 (Active control) does NOT auto-clear after read on this monitor (does on HPZ22i which clears OSD). User can keep making OSD changes after reset_vcp_x02(). - Documented behavior; manual reset_vcp_x02() called after read in MCCS<=2.1.
Power command 0xE1 uses INVERTED semantics (off=1, on=0). Sending the VESA-default 0xE1=0 to this monitor would turn it ON, not off - use 0xD6=5 (DPMS hard off) or 0xE1=1 instead.
Power command 0xE1 uses INVERTED semantics (off=1, on=0). Sending the VESA-default 0xE1=0 to this monitor would turn it ON, not off - use 0xD6=5 (DPMS hard off) or 0xE1=1 instead.
Power command 0xE1 uses INVERTED semantics (off=1, on=0). Sending the VESA-default 0xE1=0 to this monitor would turn it ON, not off - use 0xD6=5 (DPMS hard off) or 0xE1=1 instead.
Power command 0xE1 uses INVERTED semantics (off=1, on=0). Sending the VESA-default 0xE1=0 to this monitor would turn it ON, not off - use 0xD6=5 (DPMS hard off) or 0xE1=1 instead.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: Marked in static quirk table; documented but no code-side workaround.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Returns -EIO for unsupported features (except feature 0x00 returns mh=ml=sh=sl=0).
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: In ddc_output.c: when -EIO + I2C + DEL/AW3418DW, treat as DDCRC_DETERMINED_UNSUPPORTED. Hard-coded by mfg+model match.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Returns 'nominal' value 50 / max value 100 for 6-axis saturation (0x59-0x5E) and 6-axis hue (0x9B-0xA0). Spec defines nominal=127 / max=255.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: Treat 6-axis saturation/hue as standard continuous (not 'value < 127 decreases / > 127 increases'). format_feature_detail_6_axis_hue() exists but is not used.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: All-zero response when reading unsupported table feature instead of error code.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: DDCRC_READ_ALL_ZERO is treated as indication of unsupported feature, not retried.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Feature 0x52 (Active control) does NOT auto-clear after read on this monitor (does on HPZ22i which clears OSD). User can keep making OSD changes after reset_vcp_x02().
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: Documented behavior; manual reset_vcp_x02() called after read in MCCS<=2.1.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Return all-zero response (mh=ml=sh=sl=0) when reading unsupported table features.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: DDCRC_READ_ALL_ZERO treated as unsupported; flag DREF_DDC_USES_MH_ML_SH_SL_ZERO_FOR_UNSUPPORTED set.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Does not respond to single-byte read at I2C addr 0x37, but does respond to write.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: i2c_detect_x37() tries write first, falls back to read. Avoids false-negative DDC capability detection.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Calling i2c_set_addr() on the eDP I2C bus causes screen corruption.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: Never call i2c_detect_x37() / i2c_set_addr() on an eDP screen.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: ADL reports the display as supporting DDC even though it does not.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: Documentation note; covered by general 'is DDC actually working' check (DREF_DDC_COMMUNICATION_CHECKED).
Pathological-case branch in dyn_parsed_capabilities.c: if pcaps->raw_vcp_features_seen but length 0, mark capabilities as damaged.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Hot-plug disconnect is followed a few seconds later by a connect (false disconnect/connect sequence).
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: ddc_i2c_stabilized_buses() and dw_stabilized_buses_bs() insert initial_stabilization_millisec delay before processing disconnect events.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Samsung Syncmaster HID Monitor Control devices need to be recognized as monitor-control HID interfaces.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: Added to known USB monitor HID list in usb_hid_common.c.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Manufacturer uses identical generic model name across product lines and either omits product code or sets it to 0x0101. This makes Monitor_Model_Key non-unique.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: non_unique_model_id() check rejects MMK with generic_name + product_code in {0, 0x0101}. Generic names list: 'LG IPS FULLHD', 'LG UltraFine', 'LG Ultrawide', 'LG UltraWide', 'Samsung Syncmaster'.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Capabilities string violates spec multiple ways: (1) does not surround capabilities string with parens; (2) precedes segment names with a blank; (3) uses uppercase 'VCP' instead of lowercase 'vcp'.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: parse_capabilities() left-trims segment names; accepts both 'vcp' and 'VCP'; tolerates missing outer parens.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Reports usage ID==0; never returns -1 (always returns last valid value); never returns invalid string index; very slow string retrieval.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: string_id_limit = 0 disables string retrieval entirely for Apple Cinema (USB vendor 0x05ac).

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Reports VCP version 2.0 but supports feature 0x66 (Ambient light sensor) which is MCCS 2.1+.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: Documented but no special handling; ddcutil shows v21+ features when monitor advertises them via capabilities even if VCP version is older.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: Resetting feature 0x02 (New control value) turns off the OSD on this monitor (other monitors keep OSD up).
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: Documented behavior; reset_vcp_x02() called unconditionally per spec.

ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.issue: nvidia/i2c-dev driver returns EINVAL on I2C ioctl for some monitors.
ddcutilStandaloneQuirks.workaround: is_nvidia_einval_bug() detects pattern; sets nvidia_einval_bug_encountered=true; falls back to I2C_IO_STRATEGY_FILEIO for future I/O. Also doubles x37 detection wait to 2000 ms when driver=='nvidia'.
